tactical.bear_case: Below both SMA50 ($320.92) and SMA200 ($315.28) with no base formed; -19.8% drawdown can extend toward the $243 low if the op-margin compression (9.61%) is read as structural rather than transient. Knife still falling — yesterday's bounce thesis failed.
tactical.bull_case: A -19.8% drawdown in the smallest, highest-quality-trajectory prime during an active great-power-competition regime is a gift; RSI 44 leaves room to rally, and any program win or B/B beat snaps it back fast given low float volatility.
tactical.falsifier: A decisive break and hold below the $290 area (toward 52w-low retest) on accelerating volume with no catalyst would flip this to a sell/avoid; conversely a reclaim of SMA50 confirms the buy.

positional.bear_case: 9.61% op margin signals real cost/integration (Aerojet) or program-mix pressure; a drawdown to 44.5% of range with deteriorating margins is value-trap territory, and EPS revisions may be drifting negative, removing the positional tailwind entirely.
positional.bull_case: Backlog compounder with multi-year DoD visibility, trading at a real discount on forward earnings; below both MAs after a 20% pullment in a structurally bid defense sector (ITA tailwind) is exactly the entry the archetype rewards over 20d.
positional.falsifier: Latest earnings call showing book-to-bill <0.95 with negative backlog YoY, or 90d EPS revisions turning down >1%, would confirm thesis erosion and flip this positive.

strategic.bear_case: Op margin at 9.61% with supply-chain (titanium/energetics) constraints could cap the re-rating thesis; if DoD budget slips into CR/sequester uncertainty in FY27 negotiations, the defense bid fades and the cohort premium (even if trailing-distorted) reverts.
strategic.bull_case: Forward P/E discount intact, great-power-competition regime durable, Golden Dome / munitions / Aerojet propellant demand multi-year, and cohort compression toward larger primes offers structural re-rating for the smallest Big-5 name.
strategic.falsifier: A confirmed shift in DoD budget phase to sequester/CR uncertainty (failed NDAA, budget caps reinstated) or geopolitical de-escalation toward a peace dividend would flip this firmly positive (trim).

holdings.key_metric: Op margin: 9.61% (vs prime norm 14-16%) — the central swing factor; depressed trailing EPS also distorts the +33% cohort P/E premium

tactical.bear_case: 23% above SMA50 with RSI near 68 after a vertical move; any disappointment in semicap commentary or a China export headline could trigger a fast 5-8% pullback from these stretched levels.
tactical.bull_case: EUV/High-NA demand momentum is real and order flow could keep pushing the stock; momentum names like ASML routinely run RSI 70+ for weeks without reverting, so fading strength here loses.
tactical.falsifier: A clean break and hold above recent highs on accelerating volume (or a strong upside order/booking pre-announcement) would flip this to a buy-side tactical read.

positional.bear_case: The price-to-SMA50 gap of 23% is historically wide; reversion to the 50-day alone implies ~18% downside, and consolidations after such runs are common.
positional.bull_case: Backlog visibility of 2-3 years and High-NA ramp give fundamental support; the trend has been a reliable up-and-to-the-right channel and dips have been shallow and quickly bought.
positional.falsifier: A pullback of >10% toward the SMA50 that holds, or a backlog/book-to-bill acceleration, would shift positional to neutral-to-buy.

strategic.bear_case: If 2026 EPS recovery disappoints (China export controls tighten further, memory/logic capex digestion lingers), the elevated multiple compresses and the stock could derate 15-20% even with intact long-term story.
strategic.bull_case: Sole EUV supplier with widening High-NA lead ($350M+/tool), AI-driven leading-edge demand, and durable double-digit growth; at this scarcity premium the multiple can stay elevated and compound, making any neutral-to-trim score wrong.
strategic.falsifier: Forward P/E falling below ~25x (via EPS beats or a >15% drawdown) would flip strategic decisively to the buy side; conversely forward P/E pushing above 45x on flat estimates would justify a stronger trim.

holdings.key_metric: Price vs SMA50: 23% above ($1905 vs $1549) — extended after run from ~$1250

tactical.bear_case: LIN pinned at all-time highs (99.2%) with RSI 62 leaves zero cushion; any broad QUAL-factor rotation or a single soft semi-capex headline could trigger mean-reversion from a stretched technical perch, and DXY at 120 is a live FX headwind into the next print.
tactical.bull_case: Above both MAs in a confirmed golden-cross uptrend with VIX subdued; quality compounders at highs in a neutral regime tend to keep grinding higher as defensive bid persists, and there is no momentum-breaking news today.

tactical.falsifier: A 10Y move below ~4.0% (or above ~4.7%) within days, producing a clear sympathetic rally or selloff in ENB that creates a tradable tactical edge.

positional.bear_case: 56bps yield spread is genuinely thin; if the 10Y holds at 4.4%+, ENB offers little excess income and the stock can grind sideways-to-down as it has for 24 days, underperforming the book on opportunity cost.
positional.bull_case: WCS-WTI economics and Mainline throughput remain strong, LNG Canada Phase 1 ramping with Phase 2 FID optionality, and a single BoC/Fed cut compresses the required spread instantly — repricing a 4.9% yielder higher fast. The 'rich' signal is purely rate-relative, not balance-sheet driven.
positional.falsifier: 10Y falling toward 4.0% (widening yield spread back above ~100bps) or BoC/Fed initiating a cutting cycle, which would flip this to a buy.

strategic.bull_case: Enbridge is the premier North American midstream franchise — diversified across liquids, gas transmission, gas utilities and renewables, with a multi-decade secured project backlog and LNG export tailwinds. As BoC/Fed ease, a 4.9% growing, well-covered dividend re-rates and the franchise compounds steadily regardless of near-term richness.
strategic.falsifier: A credible rate-cutting cycle beginning (Fed/BoC) combined with the yield spread widening back above ~150bps, or a major new accretive LNG/gas FID — any of which would flip strategic to a buy.

holdings.key_metric: Yield spread vs 10Y: 56bps (ENB 4.94% − 10Y 4.38%) — rich, well below the 150bps cheap threshold

Funding/liquidation cascades could push price into the high-20s before any tradable low.
tactical.bull_case: RSI in low-30s at a fresh range low with strong historical 5-day bounce reliability (100% hit) sets up a sharp mean-reversion rally; crypto oversold snaps are violent and upside is uncapped. positional.falsifier: ETF flows turning decisively net-positive for a sustained stretch AND price reclaiming the 50DMA would confirm the bottom and validate stronger conviction; conversely, a sustained acceleration of net ETF OUTFLOWS with LTH distribution would flip the sign toward trim/avoid.

strategic.bear_case: If this cycle is genuinely breaking the 4-year pattern and BTC enters a structural sideways/decline regime, month-26 maturity plus a confirmed downtrend means the store-of-value narrative loses momentum and ETF AUM bleeds — a strategic trim would have been right.
strategic.bull_case: Sovereign/institutional adoption and potential regulatory tailwinds (clearer US crypto framework, possible strategic reserve discussions) extend the cycle far beyond the historical 4-year template; the strategic case is uncapped and a drawdown here is a long-term entry, not a maturity warning.
strategic.falsifier: Evidence the secular adoption curve is reversing — sustained multi-quarter ETF AUM decline plus regulatory rollback — would flip strategic to a clear trim; conversely a major positive regulatory/sovereign catalyst would push strategic negative.

holdings.key_metric: Yield spread vs 10Y: 42bps (ENB 4.97% − 10Y 4.55%) — historically very rich, well inside the <150bps threshold

tactical.bear_case: GLNCY is below its 50-day and momentum is rolling; RSI can stay in the 30s for weeks in a downtrend. Copper's rally has NOT pulled GLNCY up (3.72pp spread), suggesting name-specific selling pressure (coal ESG overhang, buyback pause fears). A break of the 200-day at $12.6 opens more downside.
tactical.bull_case: RSI 36 near oversold with price still holding above the rising 200-day is a classic dip-buy in a cyclical uptrend. COPX +3.09% and leading by 3.72pp is a genuine catch-up setup — if copper strength persists GLNCY typically snaps back within days.
tactical.falsifier: A decisive close below the 200-day SMA ($12.6) on rising volume would flip this from a dip-buy to a broken-trend sell (positive score).

positional.bear_case: The COPX-GLNCY spread has persisted for multiple sessions without convergence — that's not a coiled spring, it's the market discounting GLNCY's coal exposure and questioning the trading arm. Positional layer reliability is only 28.6%. If copper is the leading indicator and GLNCY keeps ignoring it, the mean-reversion thesis is simply wrong.
positional.bull_case: Tight HY spreads (2.67bps) + elevated GSCPI (1.25) + surging copper is a textbook cyclical-upturn positional backdrop. GLNCY at a 3.72pp discount to the copper move offers asymmetric catch-up, and the stock holds above its rising 200-day.
positional.falsifier: COPX-GLNCY spread flipping to GLNCY-leading (positive spread) while copper stalls would remove the catch-up edge and neutralize/flip the score.

strategic.bear_case: P/B 1.64 means assets are NOT cheap — the classic below-replacement strong-buy (<0.8) is absent. Coal (25% of book) faces structural ESG derating and financing headwinds; the trading arm carries recurring compliance/legal tail risk (bribery settlements). If copper rolls over on China property weakness, the trough deepens rather than recovers.
strategic.bull_case: Trough earnings (219x PE) + top-tier copper/cobalt/nickel exposure into a structural electrification demand cycle + trading arm consistently generating $2-4B floor EBITDA the market prices near zero. Any copper breakout re-rates the whole SOTP; coal cash flows fund buybacks in the meantime.
strategic.falsifier: Chinese PMI/property data deteriorating sharply and LME copper breaking down through support (dragging COPX lower) would signal the trough is deepening, flipping the strategic score positive.

holdings.key_metric: GLNCY/COPX spread: -3.72pp (COPX +3.09%, GLNCY lagging — copper catch-up potential, but spread has persisted unclosed)

tactical.bear_case: BTC is in a confirmed death-cross structure (below 50 and 200DMA), and momentum-driven assets tend to keep bleeding once trend breaks — a slide back toward the $32.84 52w low is very live if any macro or ETF-outflow catalyst hits this week.
tactical.bull_case: Price just bounced 1.45% off a base near the 52w low with RSI recovering from oversold toward the mid-40s; a short-covering / dip-buying reflex could snap it toward the 50DMA ($39.62) quickly given BTC's high beta.
tactical.falsifier: RSI breaking decisively below 35 with expanding down-volume (flips me to stronger buy) or above 60 reclaiming the 50DMA on strong ETF inflows (flips me to neutral/trim).

positional.bear_case: Positional BUY signals on this name have failed 94% of the time over 20 days during this exact drawdown; the death-cross structure plus decelerating ETF net flows means the 20-day path is more likely another leg down toward and through the $32.84 low than a durable recovery.
positional.bull_case: IBIT sits at 7.4% of its 52w range near the low with BTC 24% below the 200DMA — historically these depths, combined with the persistent structural ETF-flow bid and no forced-seller catalyst, have marked accumulation zones offering asymmetric 20-day upside.
positional.falsifier: Sustained multi-day IBIT net outflows accelerating alongside a break below $32.84 on rising volume would confirm broken structure and flip this to neutral/trim; conversely already firmly on buy side.

strategic.bear_case: If this cycle does NOT break the 4-year pattern, month 27 in extended_expansion is late-stage and strategic upside is capped by an approaching cyclical top; regulatory momentum could also stall under shifting political winds.
strategic.bull_case: Spot-ETF wrappers, corporate treasury adoption, and potential sovereign/reserve demand structurally alter the supply/demand regime — buying at ~50% off the high positions for the next multi-year expansion with uncapped upside if the old cycle framework is obsolete.
strategic.falsifier: Concrete evidence the ETF-driven demand regime is reversing (sustained cumulative net outflows across issuers, hostile regulatory reversal) would flip this to a trim; a decisive new-adoption catalyst would deepen the buy.

tactical.bear_case: Below the 50-day with fading momentum; if a soft Mexican consumer print or a peso wobble hits, the near-term path is lower and the ADR could retest the low-$100s.
tactical.bull_case: RSI in the low-40s on a quality staple often marks a short-term washout; a stable peso and mean-reversion could bounce it back toward the 50-day quickly.
tactical.falsifier: A sharp MXN move (>2% in a session) or a Banxico rate surprise that resets the short-term regime would flip this off neutral.

positional.bear_case: Mexican consumer momentum is decelerating as post-pandemic pricing tailwinds fade and real wage growth cools; volumes in Mexico/Brazil could disappoint, keeping the ADR range-bound below the 50-day.
positional.bull_case: Cheap at 12.8x P/E with a 3.76% yield and dividend growth; a stable-to-firmer peso plus continued nearshoring-driven Mexican consumer strength drives a re-rating toward the 52w high.
positional.falsifier: A negative volume/margin surprise at next earnings or a MXN break above ~18.5 would flip this positive (trim).

strategic.bear_case: Political/regulatory risk in Mexico (Sheinbaum-era policy, potential tax or sugar/health regulation), plus a strong-dollar regime if the Fed stays higher-for-longer, could compress the peso and cap USD returns for years. Nearshoring benefits are slow and uneven.
strategic.bull_case: Nearshoring structurally lifts Mexican formal employment and consumption; KOF's scale, pricing power, and digitized distribution (Juntos+) compound earnings while a stable/appreciating peso amplifies USD returns off a cheap 12.8x base.
strategic.falsifier: A durable MXN depreciation regime (toward 20+) driven by capital flight or US-Mexico trade/tariff conflict, or an adverse Mexican tax/regulatory hit to margins, would flip this to a trim.

holdings.key_metric: Trailing P/E: 12.8x — low end of KOF's historical range with 3.76% yield and stable MXN at 17.45

tactical.bear_case: Price sits below both moving averages and WTI could break lower if OPEC+ accelerates unwinding of voluntary cuts, dragging PBR near-term; any Lula fuel-pricing headline could gap it down 5%+.
tactical.bull_case: RSI mid-range leaves room to run; a single positive dividend-declaration or oil-supply-disruption headline could pop the stock several percent given its high beta and depressed positioning.
tactical.falsifier: A sharp RSI move above 60 with a decisive break back above the SMA50 ($18.69) on volume would flip this to a sell (overbought) or confirm momentum reversal.

positional.bear_case: Below-both-MAs is a downtrend, not merely oversold — driven by legitimate fear of Lula-era dividend cuts and forced low-return refining/renewables capex diverting FCF away from shareholders. The 17% gap below SMA50 could be the market pricing a structural payout reduction, not a dip to buy.
positional.bull_case: 17% below SMA50 with WTI still healthy and yield near 9.4% sets up strong mean-reversion; if the government leaves dividend policy alone into the next declaration, the discount closes quickly as EM yield-hunters return.
positional.falsifier: A confirmed board decision to materially cut ordinary dividends or a hostile capex mandate would turn the discount into a justified derating, flipping the sign positive.

strategic.bear_case: 5x P/E = peak earnings; oil rolling over from mid-cycle plus Lula's fiscal-driven appetite to tap Petrobras for social spending, force domestic fuel subsidies, and expand low-return capex all threaten the payout that IS the thesis. Brazil's deteriorating fiscal trajectory pressures BRL, compounding USD-return risk for ADR holders.
strategic.bull_case: Pre-salt breakeven near $35 means Petrobras gushes FCF at $70 oil; if Lula's team maintains fiscal discipline on the company and honors dividend policy, PBR.A is one of the cheapest high-yield EM energy plays globally, and any oil upside is levered. Peer IOCs trade at richer multiples for lower yields.
strategic.falsifier: A sustained WTI move toward/above $85 combined with an official reaffirmation of the shareholder-return/dividend framework would flip strategic to a buy; conversely a formal dividend cut confirms the trim.

holdings.key_metric: Dividend yield vs trailing P/E: 9.4% yield at 5x P/E — high yield attractive but low cyclical P/E flags peak-earnings/trim risk

tactical.bear_case: Spot rates (WCI/SCFI) rolling over as the 2024-25 capacity glut hits — Maersk itself has guided to normalization; a golden-cross into a fading rate environment is a classic bull trap and the +2.75% move fades.

tactical.falsifier: WCI composite reversing sharply higher (>10% week-over-week) on a fresh Suez closure would flip this to a buy; a break back below the 50-day on falling rates flips it to a clear trim.

positional.bear_case: GSCPI normalizes toward 0 as Suez re-opens and front-loading unwinds post-tariff-deadline; effective capacity surges, spot rates crater, and the positional tailwind the engine scored evaporates within a quarter.

positional.falsifier: GSCPI dropping below 0.5 alongside a WCI decline would flip positional negative (trim); a sustained GSCPI move above 1.75 with rising rates would push it firmly buy-side.

strategic.falsifier: P/E compressing toward 5-8x (earnings spiking to peak) or P/B rising well above 1.0 would flip this to a trim; conversely a Logistics spin-off announcement or P/B falling below 0.55 would deepen the buy.

holdings.key_metric: Price/Book: 0.70x — below fleet replacement cost, with Logistics segment implicitly valued near zero vs DSV/K+N at 15-20x EV/EBITDA

tactical.bull_case: A -13.5% drawdown in a 58-60% op-margin compounder with volumes intact is a classic setup; reclaiming the 50d with RSI in trending range and a break above the 200d at $530 would confirm momentum, and the quality bid (+1.2pp vs SPY) is actively rotating into names like MA.
